summary: Comprehensive backend (27 pytest tests) and frontend E2E (Playwright) testing of the Healthcare Marketplace. Backend: 27/27 pass — all auth, products, cart/tier pricing, order lifecycle (draft→pending_approval→approved→paid→delivered), rejection, role/org guards, and team invite endpoints work correctly. Frontend: Landing → login (bob) → marketplace filters (all/medicines/search) → product detail with tier-price recompute on qty change → add to cart → submit cart → order detail (pending_approval) → login as alice → Approve works ✓ → BUT Pay button disappears until page reload (bug). After reload, Pay works → login as eve → Deliver works. All core flows functional; one state-update bug and minor toast copy issues identified.

minor.issue: Responses do not populate buyerOrg, createdBy, approvedBy, or items.sellerOrg. GET /api/orders/:id does populate these. Because the frontend calls setOrder(data.order) after these actions, the UI loses populated fields and isBuyerOrg/isSellerOrg checks break until a page reload. Fix: in order.controller.js approveOrder/payOrder/deliverOrder/rejectOrder, re-fetch with .populate('buyerOrg','name type').populate('createdBy','name email').populate('approvedBy','name email').populate('items.product').populate('items.sellerOrg','name type') before returning.

ui_bugs.issue: After clicking Approve, the Pay button does not render until the page is manually reloaded. Root cause: backend action responses return unpopulated order, so order.buyerOrg becomes an ObjectId string — order.buyerOrg?._id is undefined — so canPay = isBuyerOrg && ... evaluates false. Same pattern affects Pay→(post-paid state for seller viewing) and Deliver. Also the header line 'Buyer: <name>' renders empty after an action because buyerOrg is no longer populated.
Toast text is grammatically wrong: uses `Order ${action}ed` -> produces 'Order approveed' and 'Order payed'. Should map action -> past tense (approved/paid/delivered/rejected).

integration_issues.issue: Each successive action button only becomes available after the user reloads the page, because setOrder is called with unpopulated backend response breaking org identity checks.

action_items: In /app/backend/src/controllers/order.controller.js, after o.save() inside approveOrder/payOrder/deliverOrder/rejectOrder, return a fully populated order (same populate chain as getOrder) instead of the raw `o`. This single fix resolves the frontend Pay-button-missing bug without any frontend changes.

Fix toast copy in /app/frontend/src/pages/OrderDetailPage.jsx line 35: replace `Order ${action}ed` with an explicit map, e.g. { approve:'Order approved', pay:'Order marked as paid', deliver:'Order marked as delivered', reject:'Order rejected' }[action].
critical_code_review_comments: order.controller.js: approveOrder/payOrder/deliverOrder/rejectOrder return the raw un-populated Mongoose doc — inconsistent with getOrder which populates. This is the root cause of the frontend state bug and also leaves the 'Buyer: <name>' header empty after an action.

cart.controller.js removeItem returns a non-populated cart (res.json({ cart })) whereas getCart/addItem/updateItem return a populated cart. Minor UX inconsistency — frontend may lose product details briefly after removal.

cart.controller.js getOrCreateDraft relies on (user.organization._id || user.organization). In register flow user.organization is set to an Org object; in login flow req.user is loaded by middleware — ensure the auth middleware consistently populates user.organization.
server.js 404 handler uses app.use('/api/*', ...). In Express 5 this pattern changed; confirm version compatibility (package.json currently uses Express 4 per observed behaviour, so this is OK).
Toast grammar bug: String interpolation `Order ${action}ed` produces non-English words like 'approveed', 'payed'.

success_rate.frontend: ~85% — all flows work, but action chain on Order page requires manual reload after each transition due to backend populate bug

test_credentials: Uses seeded accounts from /app/memory/test_credentials.md — password Password123! for alice/bob/carol/dan/eve/frank. All verified working.
seed_data_creation: Auto-seeder already populated demo data (4 orgs, 6 users, 8 products). Tests reuse seeded data and create a few TEST_ prefixed rows (new user registration, new product, invited user). Not cleaned up — safe because they are uniquely suffixed UUIDs.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend tests live at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py and pass 27/27 against the external REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL. The only open defect is unpopulated order responses on state-transition endpoints; once the main agent patches order.controller.js (approveOrder/payOrder/deliverOrder/rejectOrder) to re-fetch with the same populate chain as getOrder, re-run the Playwright chain: bob submits cart → alice approve+pay without reload → eve delivers without reload. Also verify toast copy is human-readable ('Order approved', 'Order marked as paid').
rca of the issue: Root cause: /app/backend/src/controllers/order.controller.js does NOT populate buyerOrg, createdBy, approvedBy or items.sellerOrg on the documents returned from approveOrder/payOrder/deliverOrder/rejectOrder. The frontend (OrderDetailPage.jsx) stores that response directly via setOrder(data.order), so subsequent org-identity checks (`isBuyerOrg = user.organization._id === order.buyerOrg?._id`) evaluate against undefined and return false, suppressing the next action button. Mitigation: add `.populate('buyerOrg','name type').populate('createdBy','name email').populate('approvedBy','name email').populate('items.product').populate('items.sellerOrg','name type')` after o.save() (e.g., const populated = await Order.findById(o._id).populate(...); res.json({ order: populated })). Reproduction: login bob, add item, submit; login alice, click Approve — observe Pay button is missing; reload — Pay appears. Screenshot saved at .screenshots/order-paid.png.
